metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the Autosubmit vote ACL spec: either update the parent AC to reflect the Contributors-ACL design (requester-votable, non-gating, re-verify at submit is the security mechanism) or update S1 to match bot-only ACL — one authoritative statement across parent and S1.
findings.finding: The parent AC specifies a 'bot-only vote ACL (service account `autolander-bot` / group `Autosubmit Voters`)' for the Autosubmit label, but S1 contradicts this by granting the label ACL to group `Contributors` (requesters) so agents can set it under their own identity. These two specifications are irreconcilable as written.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d: S1 — AC lines 'label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors' vs parent Phase-1 AC bullet 1 'bot-only vote ACL (service account autolander-bot / group Autosubmit Voters)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the parent AC bullet 1 to read: 'vote ACL granted to Contributors (requesters) so agents may set their own intent; the two-vote gate is not weakened because the bot re-verifies `is:submittable` on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit' — aligning with S1's design rationale and ADR-0042's security invariant argument.

findings.finding: S6 creates ADR-0042 but its ACs do not include a Security invariants section, leaving Phase-1 invariants (c) 're-verify is:submittable on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit' and (d) 'two-vote gate unweakened' without an owning story to write their concrete checks into ADR-0042. The parent Phase-1 AC explicitly requires all four invariants documented in ADR-0042 with concrete checks (a grep of project.config, a GET /projects/rebar/access diff, and/or a test asserting the re-verify logic). S1 contributes invariants (a) and (b); S8 contributes the Phase-2 SHA-binding invariant. But no story owns writing invariants (c) and (d) — including the concrete test asserting the re-verify logic — into ADR-0042's Security invariants section.
findings.location: child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) Acceptance Criteria; child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) Security-invariants AC; parent Phase 1 AC bullet 8 (Security-invariants artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC to S6: 'ADR-0042 includes a Security invariants section covering all four Phase-1 invariants — (a) non-gating Autosubmit, (b) bot-only vote ACL, (c) re-verify is:submittable on exact tested SHA before submit (with a test asserting this in S2's test suite), (d) two-vote gate unweakened — each with its concrete check. S6 assembles this section from S1's and S2's contributions and owns ensuring completeness.'.

findings.finding: The Phase 1 security-invariants AC requires 'a `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot' as proof of the gate-unchanged invariant, but no AC or described work covers *creating and storing* that pre-cutover snapshot before cutover occurs. The verification step presupposes an artifact with no criterion for its production.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC → 'Security-invariants artifact' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the security-invariants AC: 'Before pushing the `Autosubmit` label config, capture `GET /projects/rebar/access` output and commit it as `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json`; the post-cutover diff is computed against this file in CI.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either promote the CI-flake auto-recheck step into the parent AC as an explicit sub-step of the failure-handling criterion, or document in S3 that this is a deliberate scope addition with a parent-level rationale update.
findings.finding: S3 inserts a mandatory bounded auto-recheck (1 retry on the same patchset) before recording `ci_failed` and stripping Autosubmit, which is absent from the parent AC. The parent says 'CI-fail → remove `Autosubmit`, rely on the native `Verified -1` + run logs' with no recheck step. This is a scope addition in the child that contradicts the parent's prescribed flow.
findings.location: child 0525-6d71-8969-4512: S3 — AC item 'CI-flake auto-recheck (before hand-back)' vs parent Phase-1 AC failure-handling bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-bullet to the parent failure-handling AC: 'CI-fail: before recording `ci_failed`, perform one bounded auto-recheck on the same patchset (infra-failure only); only remove Autosubmit and hand back if the recheck also fails.' This aligns parent and child.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to S2 requiring `stack_id` assignment at selection time (Gerrit topic fallback to tip change-id) and add `stack_id` to S2's wipChain schema definition.
findings.finding: S3 defines `stack_id` as 'the Gerrit topic the auto-lander assigns to a stack at selection time (falling back to the tip change-id for a single-change stack)' and uses it as the primary shared key across S2/S3/S4. However, S2 (which owns stack selection) has no AC for assigning `stack_id`, and S2's wipChain schema `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase}` contains no `stack_id` field.
findings.location: child 0525-6d71-8969-4512: S3 — Outcome record section 'stack_id = the Gerrit topic the auto-lander assigns to a stack at selection time'; child 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec: S2 — wipChain schema AC
findings.suggested_fix: In S2 wipChain schema AC, extend to: `{change_id, stack_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase}` and add an AC bullet: 'At selection time, assign `stack_id`: use the change's Gerrit topic if set, otherwise the tip change-id; write `stack_id` into `wipChain` and propagate to S3 outcome records.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and assign ownership of the S3→S4 status query interface: either S3 specifies the marker file path convention S4 reads directly, or S5 adds an AC for a minimal outcome-status endpoint that S4 queries; the interface schema must appear in exactly one story's AC.
findings.finding: S4 reads the auto-lander's recorded outcome 'via its status query — a read of its state dir / a small status endpoint', but neither S3 (which owns the state dir/outcome record) nor S5 (which owns the deploy/service surface) defines this status query interface, its schema, or its owner. This is an owned-by-none interface gap.
findings.location: child 73f5-4df7-4fac-4738: S4 — AC4 '--wait termination/timeout: ... the auto-lander's recorded outcome (S3, read via its status query — a read of its state dir / a small status endpoint)'; child 0525-6d71-8969-4512: S3 — no status endpoint defined; child 3c55-2af3-0c9c-4b11: S5 — no status endpoint AC
findings.suggested_fix: In S3, add an AC: 'Expose the outcome marker as a queryable artifact: publish the marker file at a well-known path `<state_dir>/outcomes/<stack_id>.json` readable by the `land` command process; document the schema.' Remove the ambiguous 'small status endpoint' language from S4.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to S3 covering the N=5 TOCTOU re-drive exhaustion trigger from S2 as a second trigger for recording a `needs_rebase` marker, with Autosubmit removal from the whole stack.
findings.finding: S2 AC states 'after N=5 re-drives park the change and surface `needs_rebase`' but S3 owns `needs_rebase` recording (it is the authoritative persistence layer for this outcome). No AC in S2 hands off to S3's recording step on the N=5 exhaustion path, and S3's AC covers only the `409` rebase-conflict trigger — not the N=5 re-drive exhaustion trigger from S2.
findings.location: child 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec: S2 — AC 'after N=5 re-drives park the change and surface `needs_rebase` rather than casting a stale submit'; child 0525-6d71-8969-4512: S3 — AC items cover 409 rebase-conflict only for needs_rebase recording
findings.suggested_fix: In S3, add an AC bullet: 'On N=5 TOCTOU re-drive exhaustion (signaled by S2 loop reaching max retries): record a `needs_rebase` marker for the wipChain's stack, remove Autosubmit from all changes in the stack, and log the exhaustion event — same persistence and removal flow as the 409 path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the outcome enum derivation rules to specify: (1) which patchset's LLM-Review vote is authoritative for `review_failed` (current revision only); (2) what outcome is returned when LLM-Review is absent or INDETERMINATE (e.g., a fifth `gate_pending` outcome or a documented error path); (3) that TRIVIAL_REBASE vote-carry does not re-trigger `review_failed` from a prior -1.
findings.finding: The derivation rule for the `review_failed` outcome is under-specified: the plan says it is 'derived from … an `LLM-Review -1` by the land command (S4)' but does not specify which -1 qualifies (the most-recent patchset? any open vote? a -1 present at submission time?), nor what happens when the LLM-Review vote is absent or INDETERMINATE — edge cases that fall outside all four named outcome values.
findings.location: Context, Outcome Provenance note: '`merged`/`review_failed` are *derived* from native Gerrit status / an `LLM-Review -1` by the land command (S4)'; Phase 1 AC: 'ONE typed terminal outcome (`merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add S5 (`3c55-2af3-0c9c-4b11`) as an explicit dependency in S4's Dependencies section for the `lander_down` / heartbeat path.
findings.finding: S4 declares a dependency only on S1 and S3, but its `lander_down` / `6` exit-code path explicitly requires S5's heartbeat artifact ('Both read the bot heartbeat (from S5)'). S5 is an undeclared dependency of S4, creating a dependency gap: S4 cannot be fully implemented or tested before S5's heartbeat is defined.
findings.location: child 73f5-4df7-4fac-4738: S4 — AC item '`lander_down` (bot-SPOF liveness)' and 'Both read the bot heartbeat (from S5)'; S4 Dependencies section lists only S1 and S3
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S4's Dependencies: 'Also depends on S5 (`3c55-2af3-0c9c-4b11`) for the heartbeat artifact consumed by the `lander_down` exit path (AC2).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike or RED-test fixture (e.g., rebase a test change via the REST API with `on_behalf_of_uploader` and assert all trailers are preserved verbatim) before implementing the full serial lander, to empirically validate the Gerrit trailer-preservation behavior.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `on_behalf_of_uploader` on `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` (and `/rebase:chain`) will preserve DCO sign-off and `rebar-ticket` trailers, but no spike, RED-test fixture, or probe is specified to validate this Gerrit REST API integration point before full build-out.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'on_behalf_of_uploader to preserve author/DCO/rebar-ticket trailers'
findings.suggested_fix: In the story that implements the rebase step, add a prerequisite probe: use the autolander-bot service account to rebase a synthetic change on the staging Gerrit instance and assert `Signed-off-by`, `rebar-ticket`, and `Change-Id` trailers survive unchanged. Gate the story's implementation on a green probe result.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (via a live Gerrit 3.14.1 spike or documented API reference) that `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves the full commit message including `Signed-off-by` and `rebar-ticket` trailers; add the verification result to ADR-0042's Security invariants section.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` (and `/rebase:chain`) with `on_behalf_of_uploader` will preserve DCO `Signed-off-by` trailers and `rebar-ticket` trailers on the rebased commits. This is an unverified Gerrit 3.14.1 REST API capability: Gerrit's server-side rebase rewrites the committer field but its handling of the commit *message* (where trailers live) under `on_behalf_of_uploader` is not confirmed by any spike, test fixture, or codebase evidence. If Gerrit modifies or strips trailers during server-side rebase, the DCO requirement (`receive.requireSignedOffBy = true`) could be violated or produce unexpected behavior when the rebased patchset is re-submitted.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: '`on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'
findings.suggested_fix: Run a throwaway spike on the live Gerrit instance: push a test change with a `Signed-off-by` and a `rebar-ticket` trailer, trigger a server-side rebase via the REST API with `on_behalf_of_uploader`, and inspect the resulting patchset's commit message. Document the result in ADR-0042. If trailers are NOT preserved, the bot must apply them explicitly post-rebase (amend the commit message via `PUT /changes/{id}/message` or use the `rebase` API's `committerEmail` / message fields if available).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Disambiguate '(S3)' in the Outcome Provenance paragraph — spell it out as either 'AWS S3 bucket' or 'Story S3 (state-file story)' so an executor can act without clarifying.
findings.finding: The notation '(S3)' in the Outcome Provenance sentence is ambiguous: it could mean AWS S3 object storage (used elsewhere in the project for bot state) or Story 3 of this epic. An executor choosing the wrong interpretation would build the wrong persistence backend.
findings.location: Context section — Outcome provenance paragraph: 'the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(S3)' with '(persisted to the bot's S3 state bucket, as in the review-bot pattern)' — or, if it means a story reference, '(Story S3)' with a note that Story S3 defines the persistence schema.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define `wipChain` in the plan: specify the concrete mechanism (e.g., an in-process mutex/queue, a DB row, a file lock) that enforces 'one active landing operation at a time' and what state it holds.
findings.finding: The term 'wipChain' is used as a load-bearing design term ('Single wipChain (serialized, one instance)') but is never defined — an executor cannot determine whether it is a lock file, an in-memory singleton, a database row, a Gerrit WIP-state annotation, or a queue entry.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC bullet: 'Serial auto-lander service … Single `wipChain` (serialized, one instance).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default polling interval and maximum wait timeout for `--wait` (e.g., poll every 60 s, give up after 90 min) so the executor doesn't have to guess.
findings.finding: The `--wait` blocking variant's polling interval and timeout are not specified, leaving an executor to choose arbitrary values that may conflict with GHA job duration, Gerrit API rate limits, or the project's cost/attention targets.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Agent landing contract bullet: 'blocking (`--wait`) and single-shot status variants'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'polls every 60 s; gives up and returns `ci_failed` after the project's standard CI timeout (currently ~60 min) + a grace period; the timeout is configurable via an env var.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the required Gerrit ACL grant for `on_behalf_of_uploader` to the Autosubmit label / service-account setup AC (e.g., grant `autolander-bot` the `Rebase On Behalf Of` or equivalent capability in `project.config`).
findings.finding: The required Gerrit ACL permissions for the service account to use `on_behalf_of_uploader` on the rebase API are not specified. This is a non-trivial Gerrit ACL that requires explicit `rebase` capability or `Submit` on behalf of others; without it, the rebase call will return 403 and the bot cannot function.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'rebases … `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exit-code mapping explicitly (e.g., 0=merged, 1=needs_rebase, 2=ci_failed, 3=review_failed — or state 'non-zero for all non-merged; read stdout for type') so scripted consumers have a complete, deterministic contract.
findings.finding: The AC states 'distinct exit code' for the four typed outcomes but does not specify the mapping — whether each of the four outcomes (merged|needs_rebase|ci_failed|review_failed) maps to a unique exit code, or whether only success/failure is distinguished (0 vs non-zero, with the outcome type in stdout). This is a minor defaultable gap that affects downstream script consumers.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Agent landing contract bullet: 'ONE typed terminal outcome … + distinct exit code'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small table: exit 0 = merged; exit 1 = needs_rebase; exit 2 = ci_failed; exit 3 = review_failed. Mirrors the git-bisect convention of distinct codes per outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the 'single source of truth' claim with the outcome-provenance split: either (a) clarify that 'single source of truth' means 'the land command is the single interface' (the bot's state + Gerrit together feed it, and that's by design), or (b) add a covering AC that verifies the land command correctly assembles all four outcomes from their respective sources with defined precedence.
findings.finding: The AC states 'The bot is the single source of truth for the outcome,' but the Context outcome-provenance note states that `merged` and `review_failed` outcomes are 'derived from native Gerrit status / an LLM-Review -1 by the land command' — not recorded in bot state. These two sections contradict each other: the bot cannot be the single source of truth if the land command derives two of the four outcomes directly from Gerrit, bypassing bot state.
findings.location: Context → 'Outcome provenance' note vs AC Phase 1 → 'Agent landing contract' bullet ('The bot is the single source of truth for the outcome')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a clarifying sentence to the AC bullet: 'Single source of truth means the land command is the sole agent-facing interface; it assembles outcomes from two backing stores — bot state (S3) for `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed`, and native Gerrit status for `merged`/`review_failed` — with a defined precedence rule (e.g. bot-recorded outcome wins over live Gerrit if both are set).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure for the `refs/meta/config` push (e.g., a revert-commit recipe or admin script) to the plan/ADR-0042, so operators can restore the pre-cutover `project.config` state if the `Autosubmit` label ACL is misconfigured.
findings.finding: The push of the new `Autosubmit` label to `refs/meta/config` is a live, privileged, immediately-effective change to Gerrit's submit rules and ACLs. The plan cites a 'pre-cutover snapshot' for security auditing but specifies no rollback procedure — no revert commit recipe, no tested back-out script, and no description of what 'roll back' means if the ACL is wrong (e.g., non-bot accounts gain submit, or submits are blocked for all changes). Under T4, an explicit rollback/back-out plan is required, not just acknowledgement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria → Phase 1, bullet 1: '`Autosubmit` Gerrit label authored in `infra/gerrit/project.config` / pushed to `refs/meta/config`'; Security-invariants bullet: 'GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot'
findings.suggested_fix: In ADR-0042's Security Invariants section, add a 'Rollback' sub-section: (1) store the pre-cutover `refs/meta/config` SHA as a documented baseline; (2) provide a `git push origin <baseline-sha>:refs/meta/config` revert recipe; (3) specify that the `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff is run post-push AND post-revert to confirm restoration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the sequencing between the CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md rewrite and the lander going live (atomic cutover or expand-contract), and add a doc-rollback note tied to the lander rollback procedure so the old manual-submit instructions can be restored if the lander is reverted.
findings.finding: CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md are described as a 'rewrite' — a destructive replacement of existing landing guidance that in-flight agents and contributors are actively following. The plan does not specify when the doc rewrite happens relative to the lander going live, nor does it provide an expand-contract sequence (e.g., deprecation notice in old docs, or atomic cutover). Agents mid-flight on the old manual workflow during the transition window receive no guidance on which procedure applies.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria → Phase 1, Docs bullet: 'Docs: rewrite this repo's landing section (`CLAUDE.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md`) to the new contract'
findings.suggested_fix: Mark the doc rewrite as the final story in the epic (or the same story as the lander deployment), make it conditional on the lander passing its own acceptance tests, and keep the old landing section in a `## Legacy manual submit (deprecated)` block until the lander is confirmed stable — then remove in a follow-up commit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly flag the poll interval, back-off, and CI-wait timeout for 'awaiting fresh Verified' as configurable parameters (e.g., environment variables in the compose spec) rather than hardcoded constants, so they can be tuned without a code change.
findings.finding: The polling cadence / retry interval for 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' is unspecified and not flagged as a configurable parameter, yet it is a threshold likely to need tuning as GHA queue times vary.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'awaits a fresh Verified +1'
findings.suggested_fix: In the autolander service spec (or the relevant child story), add: `AUTOLANDER_POLL_INTERVAL_S` (default 30), `AUTOLANDER_CI_TIMEOUT_S` (default 3600), and `AUTOLANDER_MAX_RETRIES` as env-var-driven config, documented in `infra/compose/`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename the epic title to accurately reflect committed scope (serial lander only), e.g. 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — serial lander (project infrastructure)', and move the 'serial→batching' framing into a 'Phase roadmap' note within the Context section where the deferral is already explained.
findings.finding: The plan title 'serial->batching' implies batching is part of the committed deliverable, but the body explicitly rejects cross-stack batching (Phase 2 deferred to idea c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27). A reader encountering only the title or roadmap entry would expect batching to be delivered.
findings.location: Plan title: 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — serial->batching (project infrastructure)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change title to 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — Phase 1: serial lander (project infrastructure)' and add a one-line note in Context: 'Phase 2 (batching/bounded speculation) is tracked separately in idea c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Distinguish a rebase-conflict 409 (content conflict → hand back to agent as `needs_rebase`) from a submit-race 409 (another change landed between Verified +1 and the submit call → lander re-runs the rebase→await→submit cycle automatically, without agent involvement).
findings.finding: The plan conflates two structurally different HTTP 409 cases — a rebase-conflict 409 (from POST /changes/{id}/rebase when the content conflicts) and a submit-race 409 (from POST /changes/{id}/submit when another change landed between Verified arriving and the submit call) — into a single 'remove Autosubmit and hand back to agent' outcome. A submit-race 409 is not a conflict; the change content is clean and needs only another rebase→await→submit cycle. Handing back to the agent in this case violates the epic's own primary success criterion ('zero further agent attention in the common non-conflicting case').
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Failure handling bullet: 'rebase-conflict (409) → remove Autosubmit and record a conflict outcome'
findings.suggested_fix: In the failure-handling AC, split the 409 case: (a) 409 on POST /changes/{id}/rebase → rebase conflict → remove Autosubmit, record needs_rebase, hand back to agent; (b) 409 on POST /changes/{id}/submit → submit race → the lander automatically re-rebases to the new tip and re-runs the await→submit cycle (no agent interaction, counted against a per-stack retry budget).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the selection predicate: the lander selects the front change/chain that has `Autosubmit` set (regardless of current submittability), then rebases it to tip, awaits Verified, then submits — 'submittable' in the AC should describe the goal state after the cycle, not a precondition for selection.
findings.finding: The selection predicate 'front Autosubmit+submittable change/chain' is ambiguous in a critical way: under FFO, a chain is only 'submittable' (is:submittable) when it sits on the current main tip AND both gate votes are MAX. After the lander rebases a chain, Verified drops (strict copyCondition), making the chain NOT submittable until CI re-runs and re-votes. If an implementer reads 'submittable' as a selection filter (pick chains that are ALREADY is:submittable), the lander would only act on chains already at tip with both votes green — the rare pre-cooked case — and would never rebase chains that need it, defeating the core automation purpose.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander bullet: 'for the front Autosubmit+submittable change/chain it rebases to the current tip'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the AC bullet to: 'for the front change/chain with an Autosubmit vote set, the lander: (1) rebases to the current tip, (2) awaits a fresh Verified +1 on the rebased patchset, (3) re-verifies is:submittable on the exact tested SHA, then (4) submits ancestor-atomically.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare a new SSM SecureString slot (e.g. `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token`) in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, add it to `fetch-secrets.sh`'s curated map, and reference it in the autolander compose service's `env_file` — mirroring the existing `gerrit-bot-token` pattern for the review-bot.
findings.finding: No SSM SecureString slot is declared for the `autolander-bot` Gerrit API credential. The review-bot pattern requires `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` → ssm.tf → fetch-secrets.sh → .env. The autolander-bot needs its own Gerrit REST API token (to call POST /rebase, PATCH label, POST /submit, etc.) but no analogous SSM slot or fetch-secrets.sh mapping is specified anywhere in the plan or AC, creating a gap where the credential is undeclared in IaC and its delivery path to the container is unspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Phase 1, second bullet ('Serial auto-lander service … infra/compose/Dockerfile.* + compose'); Context section ('autolander-bot / group Autosubmit Voters')
findings.suggested_fix: In ssm.tf, add `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token` to the `rebar_secret_params` local list. In fetch-secrets.sh, map it to `AUTOLANDER_BOT_TOKEN`. In docker-compose.yml, reference it in the autolander service's `env_file: .env` block, same as review-bot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the autolander outcome state store in IaC: if AWS S3, add a `aws_s3_bucket` resource with encryption, versioning, and a scoped `s3:GetObject`/`s3:PutObject` inline policy on the instance role; if a local file, add a named compose volume (e.g. `autolander_state`) bound to an EBS-backed host path in `docker-compose.yml`, mirroring the `gerrit_reviewbot` volume pattern.
findings.finding: The plan references the autolander bot recording `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` outcomes in 'its own state (S3)' but no IaC — no S3 bucket resource, no Terraform resource, no compose named volume, no SSM slot — is specified for this state store. The state store is left as an undeclared, out-of-code artifact whose durability, IAM access, and teardown path are all unspecified.
findings.location: Context section — 'Outcome provenance: the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3)'; Acceptance Criteria Phase 1 — 'the bot is the single source of truth for the outcome'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a CloudWatch metric + alarm for autolander health (e.g. a heartbeat metric published by the autolander's poll loop to `rebar/autolander:loop_heartbeat`, with a `treat_missing_data=breaching` alarm), following the pattern of `monitoring_autodeploy.tf` and wired to the existing SNS alerts topic.
findings.finding: No CloudWatch alarm is specified for the autolander service being down, stuck in a loop, or silently failing. The plan says container stdout goes to 'existing CloudWatch monitoring', but 'existing' alarms (monitoring.tf, monitoring_autodeploy.tf) watch Gerrit reachability and autodeploy errors — none watches the autolander. A crashed or wedged autolander would be invisible until an agent notices a change was never landed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Phase 1, Observability bullet: 'the bot logs to its own service logs (container stdout → existing CloudWatch monitoring)'
findings.suggested_fix: Have the autolander's main loop publish `rebar/autolander:loop_heartbeat = 1` via `cloudwatch:PutMetricData` each iteration. Add a `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm.autolander_down` (treat_missing_data=breaching, threshold < 1, 2 consecutive 5-min periods) in a new `infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf`, wired to `aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the `autolander-bot` credential lifecycle explicitly: add an SSM SecureString slot (e.g. `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token`), extend `fetch-secrets.sh` and `ssm.tf` to provision and rotate it following the existing `gerrit-bot-token` pattern, and name the resulting env-var in the compose service definition.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `autolander-bot` Gerrit service account but does not declare how its credential (HTTP token or SSH key) is provisioned, stored, or rotated. The existing pattern for the review-bot credential is SSM SecureString (`/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`) → `fetch-secrets.sh` → `.env` (0600). The plan is silent on whether a new SSM parameter slot is added, whether `fetch-secrets.sh` and `ssm.tf` are extended, and how the running container receives the credential. This is an undeclared secret lifecycle on a new boundary-crossing service account.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / Phase 1, AC #2: 'Serial auto-lander service … service account `autolander-bot`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token` to `local.rebar_secret_params` in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`; add a `get_param autolander-bot-token` call in `fetch-secrets.sh` writing `AUTOLANDER_BOT_TOKEN`; reference `AUTOLANDER_BOT_TOKEN` from the new autolander compose service's `env_file`. Include the autolander-bot token in the ADR-0042 Security invariants section as a managed secret.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the Gerrit ACL grant that enables `on_behalf_of_uploader` for `autolander-bot`: name the `[access]` section, the group, and any scope constraint (e.g. project-level vs site-level), and include this grant in the ADR-0042 Security invariants section with a concrete verification check.
findings.finding: The plan uses `on_behalf_of_uploader` on `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` and `/rebase:chain`, which requires the `autolander-bot` account to hold Gerrit's 'Run As' (impersonation) ACL permission. The plan does not specify which `[access]` section in `project.config` (or `All-Projects`) grants this permission, what its scope is, or that it is constrained to the minimum necessary operation. An overly broad 'Run As' grant would allow the bot to impersonate any Gerrit user for any operation on any ref.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / Phase 1, AC #2: '`on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `wipChain` record schema inline in the plan: enumerate its fields (e.g. `change_ids`, `phase: Literal['rebasing','awaiting_verified','submitting']`, `last_updated`, `owner_agent_id`), list all valid phases, and specify the transition rules so the service implementer and the `land-status` command implementer share a single source of truth.
findings.finding: The `wipChain` state record is referenced throughout (serialized service state, one instance, drives the rebase→verify→submit phases) but its schema — field names, phase vocabulary, and valid phase transitions — is never defined anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Context (Design section): 'Single `wipChain` (serialized, one instance)'; Phase 1 AC bullet for the serial lander service; Greenfield note: 'the autolander service + its `wipChain` and state dir … are all NEW artifacts'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## wipChain schema` subsection to the Context or a dedicated story that defines the record as a typed dict (e.g. `{change_ids: list[str], phase: 'rebasing'|'awaiting_verified'|'submitting'|'done'|'failed', failure_reason: 'ci_failed'|'needs_rebase'|None, started_at: ISO8601, updated_at: ISO8601}`) and state that the `land-status` command reads from this record.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the outcome-record schema in the plan (or a dedicated schema appendix): specify field names, types, and allowed values for at least `change_id`, `outcome: 'needs_rebase'|'ci_failed'`, `recorded_at`, and any retrieval key (e.g., Change-Id), so the bot writer and the `land-status` command writer share a single spec.
findings.finding: The `outcome-record schema` is declared as a NEW artifact in the Greenfield note but is never structurally defined: no field names, no type vocabulary, no storage key format, and no description of how `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` are encoded when the bot 'records' them.
findings.location: Greenfield note: 'the outcome-record schema … are all NEW artifacts created by this epic's stories'; Context Outcome Provenance: 'the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Disambiguate '(S3)'/'(S4)' in the Outcome Provenance note: if these are story references, write 'Story S3'/'Story S4'; if they reference AWS S3 storage, write 'AWS S3 bucket' and name the target bucket or reference the existing state-backend configuration.
findings.finding: The Outcome Provenance notation '(S3)' and '(S4)' is ambiguous: in this project 'S3' is a well-established abbreviation for AWS S3 object storage (the bot's state backend), while the plan also uses S-numbers (S1–S9) for epic stories. An executor cannot determine whether 'the bot records `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3)' means AWS S3 bucket storage or Story 3.
findings.location: Context, Outcome Provenance note: 'the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3); `merged`/`review_failed` are *derived* from native Gerrit status / an `LLM-Review -1` by the land command (S4)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(S3)' with 'Story S3 (the autolander service story)' and '(S4)' with 'Story S4 (the land command story)', or if AWS S3 is intended, write 'the autolander's AWS S3 state bucket' and reference the existing bucket config.

findings.finding: S2's 'bounded re-drive attempts' for the FFO TOCTOU guard is stated in both the parent AC and S2's AC without a concrete numeric bound, making the criterion unmeasurable and untestable. Every other bounded-retry mechanism in the epic specifies a concrete cap (S7: N=5 re-batch attempts; S9: N=3 re-batch attempts per stack). S2's absence of a number means there is no acceptance test that can verify the loop terminates under pathological tip-advance churn.
findings.location: child 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec (S2) AC bullet 4 ('looping (bounded re-drive attempts) rather than casting a stale submit'); parent Phase 1 AC bullet 2 ('bounded re-drive attempts')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend S2 AC bullet 4: '...looping (bounded to **N=5** re-drive attempts); on exhaustion, delegate to S3's failure path: remove Autosubmit from all stack members and record a needs_rebase outcome.'

findings.finding: The parent's Phase 1 'Security-invariants artifact' AC explicitly requires ADR-0042 to record the 're-verify is:submittable on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit' invariant and its concrete check (a test asserting the re-verify logic). S2 specifies the FFO TOCTOU guard as loop logic, but S2 makes no mention of recording it in ADR-0042. S6 authors the ADR but does not enumerate specific security invariants it must include (only 'records the auto-lander, agent contract, staged plan, separation-of-concerns'). S1 covers the label invariants in ADR-0042; S8 covers the Phase 2 SHA-binding invariant. No child owns the Phase 1 re-verify invariant entry in the ADR-0042 Security invariants section — this is a partial-without-remainder gap.
findings.location: child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) and child 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec (S2)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC to S6: 'ADR-0042 Security invariants section includes the Phase 1 re-verify-is:submittable-on-tested-SHA invariant, citing the S2 test that asserts this logic.' Alternatively add an AC to S2: 'Document the FFO TOCTOU guard in the ADR-0042 Security invariants section with a concrete grep/test citation.'

findings.finding: The autolander state dir is written by four separate stories (S3, S7, S8, S9) with four distinct record schemas and no story defining the overall directory layout, filename conventions, write ordering, or file-level locking for concurrent Phase-2 writes. S9 adds a retry counter and parked boolean flag to the state dir without specifying whether they extend S3's outcome record or live in separate files; S8 writes a SHA-binding map; S7 writes a handoff record. On crash/restart across Phase-2 components, recovery depends on reading multiple partially-written files with no holistic consistency contract.
findings.location: child 0525-6d71-8969-4512 (S3) Outcome record section; child 0128-9a17-79db-450e (S7) AC bullet 4 (handoff record); child 23f7-b1b7-270e-4bed (S8) AC bullet 1 (SHA-binding map); child cf10-0423-e0de-464b (S9) AC bullet 6 (retry counter + parked flag)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend S3's scope to own the full state dir schema definition (a single `state/` directory with named subdirectories or files: `outcomes/<stack_id>.json`, `batches/<batch_id>/handoff.json`, `batches/<batch_id>/sha-map.json`, `batches/<batch_id>/retry-state.json`), and require S7/S8/S9 to conform to that schema rather than independently defining their own file layouts.

findings.finding: The `wipChain` state machine — named as a NEW artifact central to the serial auto-lander — has no state vocabulary, transition table, or reset/idle protocol defined in this epic plan. The plan mentions `wipChain` (serialized, one instance) and 'RETIRE/reset' semantics implicitly in child-story references, but the epic never defines what states wipChain can be in, what events drive transitions, or what state it holds after a successful submit.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Serial auto-lander service' bullet; Greenfield note — 'the autolander service + its `wipChain` and state dir … are all NEW artifacts created by this epic's stories'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
